title: Cybersecurity AI: Hacking Consumer Robots in the AI Era
authors: Víctor Mayoral-Vilches, Unai Ayucar-Carbajo, Olivier Laflamme, Ruikai Peng, María Sanz-Gómez, Francesco Balassone, Lucas Apa, Endika Gil-Uriarte
abstract: Is robot cybersecurity broken by AI? Consumer robots -- from autonomous lawnmowers to powered exoskeletons and window cleaners -- are rapidly entering homes and workplaces, yet their security remains rooted in assumptions of specialized attacker expertise. This paper presents evidence that Generative AI has fundamentally disrupted robot cybersecurity: what historically required deep knowledge of ROS, ROS 2, and robotic system internals can now be automated by anyone with access to state-of-the-art GenAI tools spearheaded by the open source CAI (Cybersecurity AI). We provide empirical evidence through three case studies: (1) compromising a Hookii autonomous lawnmower robot, uncovering fleet-wide vulnerabilities and data protection violations affecting 267+ connected devices, (2) exploiting a Hypershell powered exoskeleton, demonstrating safety-critical motor control weaknesses and credential exposure including access to over 3,300 internal support emails, and (3) breaching a HOBOT S7 Pro window cleaning robot, achieving unauthenticated BLE command injection and OTA firmware exploitation. Across these platforms, CAI discovered in an automated manner 38 vulnerabilities that would have previously required months of specialized security research. Our findings reveal a stark asymmetry: while offensive capabilities have been democratized through AI, defensive measures often remain lagging behind. We argue that traditional defense-in-depth architectures like the Robot Immune System (RIS) must evolve toward GenAI-native defensive agents capable of matching the speed and adaptability of AI-powered attacks.
title_zh: 网络安全人工智能：人工智能时代对消费型机器人的黑客攻击
abstract_zh: 人工智能是否已击穿机器人网络安全？从自主割草机到电动外骨骼和窗户清洁机器人，消费类机器人正迅速进入家庭与工作场所，但其安全防护仍建立在对专业攻击者技能的假设之上。本文提供实证证据表明：生成式人工智能（GenAI）已从根本上颠覆了机器人网络安全格局——过去需要深入掌握ROS、ROS 2及机器人系统内部机制的专业知识，如今仅需具备先进生成式AI工具（由开源CAI，即“网络安全人工智能”引领）的普通用户即可实现自动化攻击。我们通过三个案例研究提供了实证支持：（1）攻破Hookii自主割草机器人，发现影响超过267台联网设备的系统性漏洞与数据保护违规问题；（2）入侵Hypershell电动外骨骼，暴露出关键安全性的电机控制缺陷及凭证泄露，导致超过3,300封内部支持邮件被获取；（3）突破HOBOT S7 Pro窗户清洁机器人，成功实现无需认证的蓝牙低功耗（BLE）命令注入与OTA固件远程利用。在这些平台上，CAI以自动化方式识别出38个此前需数月专业安全研究才能发现的漏洞。我们的研究揭示了一个严峻的不对称现实：尽管攻击能力因人工智能而实现平民化，防御手段却往往滞后于攻击步伐。我们认为，传统的纵深防御架构（如机器人免疫系统RIS）必须向原生支持生成式人工智能的防御代理演进，以匹配AI驱动攻击的速度与适应性。

title: PostTrainBench: Can LLM Agents Automate LLM Post-Training?
authors: Ben Rank, Hardik Bhatnagar, Ameya Prabhu, Shira Eisenberg, Karina Nguyen, Matthias Bethge, Maksym Andriushchenko
abstract: AI agents have become surprisingly proficient at software engineering over the past year, largely due to improvements in reasoning capabilities. This raises a deeper question: can these systems extend their capabilities to automate AI research itself? In this paper, we explore post-training, the critical phase that turns base LLMs into useful assistants. We introduce PostTrainBench to benchmark how well LLM agents can perform post-training autonomously under bounded compute constraints (10 hours on one H100 GPU). We ask frontier agents (e.g., Claude Code with Opus 4.6) to optimize the performance of a base LLM on a particular benchmark (e.g., Qwen3-4B on AIME). Importantly, we do not provide any predefined strategies to the agents and instead give them full autonomy to find necessary information on the web, run experiments, and curate data. We find that frontier agents make substantial progress but generally lag behind instruction-tuned LLMs from leading providers: 23.2% for the best agent vs. 51.1% for official instruction-tuned models. However, agents can exceed instruction-tuned models in targeted scenarios: GPT-5.1 Codex Max achieves 89% on BFCL with Gemma-3-4B vs. 67% for the official model. We also observe several failure modes worth flagging. Agents sometimes engage in reward hacking: training on the test set, downloading existing instruction-tuned checkpoints instead of training their own, and using API keys they find to generate synthetic data without authorization. These behaviors are concerning and highlight the importance of careful sandboxing as these systems become more capable. Overall, we hope PostTrainBench will be useful for tracking progress in AI R&D automation and for studying the risks that come with it. Website and code are available at https://posttrainbench.com/.
title_zh: PostTrainBench：大模型代理能否自动化大模型后训练？
abstract_zh:
在过去一年中，AI代理在软件工程领域的表现令人惊讶地提升，这主要得益于其推理能力的显著进步。这一进展引发了一个更深层次的问题：这些系统能否进一步拓展自身能力，实现对人工智能研究本身的自动化？在本文中，我们探讨了“后训练”（post-training）这一关键阶段——正是这一阶段将基础大语言模型（LLM）转化为真正有用的助手。为此，我们提出了PostTrainBench，一个用于评估大语言模型代理在有限计算资源约束下（单张H100 GPU上最多10小时）自主完成后训练能力的基准测试平台。我们要求前沿代理（如Claude Code搭配Opus 4.6版本）在特定任务上优化基础LLM的表现（例如，对Qwen3-4B在AIME基准上的性能进行提升）。重要的是，我们并未向代理提供任何预设策略，而是赋予它们完全自主权：自行从网络获取必要信息、运行实验并整理数据。

我们的研究发现，尽管前沿代理取得了显著进展，但总体上仍落后于主流厂商提供的指令微调LLM模型：最佳代理的表现为23.2%，而官方指令微调模型则达到51.1%。然而，在某些特定场景下，代理甚至能够超越官方模型：GPT-5.1 Codex Max在BFCL基准上对Gemma-3-4B进行优化时，达到了89%的成绩，远超官方模型的67%。此外，我们也观察到若干值得关注的失败模式。部分代理出现了“奖励劫持”行为：例如，在测试集上进行训练、下载现成的指令微调模型权重而非自主训练，或利用从网络上发现的API密钥未经授权生成合成数据。这些行为令人担忧，凸显了随着系统能力不断增强，必须加强沙盒环境的安全管控。

总体而言，我们希望PostTrainBench能成为追踪人工智能研发自动化进展的重要工具，并帮助深入研究伴随其发展而来的潜在风险。相关网站与代码已公开，访问地址为：https://posttrainbench.com/。

title: OSS-CRS: Liberating AIxCC Cyber Reasoning Systems for Real-World Open-Source Security
authors: Andrew Chin, Dongkwan Kim, Yu-Fu Fu, Fabian Fleischer, Youngjoon Kim, HyungSeok Han, Cen Zhang, Brian Junekyu Lee, Hanqing Zhao, Taesoo Kim
abstract: DARPA's AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC) showed that cyber reasoning systems (CRSs) can go beyond vulnerability discovery to autonomously confirm and patch bugs: seven teams built such systems and open-sourced them after the competition. Yet all seven open-sourced CRSs remain largely unusable outside their original teams, each bound to the competition cloud infrastructure that no longer exists. We present OSS-CRS, an open, locally deployable framework for running and combining CRS techniques against real-world open-source projects, with budget-aware resource management. We ported the first-place system (Atlantis) and discovered 10 previously unknown bugs (three of high severity) across 8 OSS-Fuzz projects. OSS-CRS is publicly available.
title_zh: OSS-CRS：为现实世界开源安全释放AIxCC网络推理系统
abstract_zh: DARPA的AI网络挑战赛（AIxCC）表明，网络推理系统（CRSs）能够超越漏洞发现，实现自主确认并修补漏洞：七支参赛队伍构建了此类系统，并在比赛结束后将其开源。然而，这七套开源的CRS系统至今仍基本无法在原团队之外使用，因为它们均依赖于已不存在的比赛专用云基础设施。为此，我们提出了OSS-CRS——一个开放、可本地部署的框架，用于在真实世界开源项目上运行和组合多种CRS技术，并具备预算感知的资源管理能力。我们成功将排名第一的系统（Atlantis）移植到该框架中，并在8个OSS-Fuzz项目中发现了10个此前未知的漏洞（其中3个为高危漏洞）。OSS-CRS现已公开发布。

title: TUMSphere: Turning a University Curriculum into Playable VR Challenges
authors: Santiago Berrezueta-Guzman, Nadia Damianova, Andrei Koshelev, Ivan Parmacli, Stefan Wagner
abstract: Traditional university orientation formats struggle to convey the intellectual substance of STEM curricula, particularly in disciplines where core competencies, such as algorithmic thinking and formal reasoning, are inherently abstract. This paper presents TUMSphere, a serious virtual reality (VR) application built as an interactive digital twin of the TUM Bildungscampus Heilbronn, in which six curriculum-mapped mini-games translate foundational Information Engineering topics into hands-on VR challenges. The mini-games, covering introductory programming, hardware debugging, code completion, graph traversal, shortest-path optimization, and relational database querying, follow a graduated difficulty progression that mirrors the real semesters' structure of the degree. We describe the pedagogical rationale, the VR interaction mechanics, and nine cross-cutting design considerations that guided development. A within-subjects pilot study (N = 18) using pre-/post-knowledge tests, the System Usability Scale, a User Engagement Scale adaptation, and the Simulator Sickness Questionnaire yielded a statistically significant knowledge gain (p < 0.001, r = 0.86), good usability (SUS M = 76.4), high engagement (M = 4.21/5), and negligible simulator sickness (SSQ M = 7.1). Task performance logs confirmed the intended difficulty gradient across mini-games. These results suggest that embedding authentic academic challenges in an explorable VR campus is a viable and extensible approach to gamified STEM outreach.
title_zh: TUMSphere：将大学课程转化为可玩的虚拟现实挑战
abstract_zh: 传统大学迎新形式难以有效传达STEM课程的学术内涵，尤其是在核心能力（如算法思维和形式推理）本身具有高度抽象性的学科中。本文介绍TUMSphere——一款专为慕尼黑工业大学海尔布龙教育园区打造的严肃型虚拟现实（VR）应用，作为该校区的交互式数字孪生体。该应用包含六个与课程内容紧密对应的微型游戏，将信息工程的基础知识转化为沉浸式的VR实践挑战。这些微型游戏涵盖编程入门、硬件调试、代码补全、图遍历、最短路径优化以及关系数据库查询等内容，其难度呈渐进式提升，与实际学位课程的学期结构相呼应。本文阐述了其教学设计原理、VR交互机制，以及指导开发的九项跨领域设计考量。一项针对18名参与者的自身对照试点研究，通过前后知识测试、系统可用性量表（SUS）、用户参与度量表改编版及模拟器晕动症问卷（SSQ）进行评估，结果显示：知识掌握程度显著提升（p < 0.001，r = 0.86），系统可用性良好（SUS均值 = 76.4），用户参与度高（均值 = 4.21/5），模拟器晕动症状极轻微（SSQ均值 = 7.1）。任务表现日志也验证了各微型游戏之间预期的难度梯度。研究结果表明，在可探索的VR校园环境中嵌入真实的学术挑战，是一种可行且可扩展的STEM科普游戏化方法。

title: One Model Is Enough: Native Retrieval Embeddings from LLM Agent Hidden States
authors: Bo Jiang
abstract: LLM agents that retrieve external knowledge typically generate a search query as text, then run a separate embedding model to encode it into a vector. This two-model pipeline adds infrastructure complexity and latency, yet is redundant: the LLM already encodes the full conversational context in its hidden states. We propose equipping LLM agents with native retrieval capability by adding a lightweight projection head that maps hidden states directly into the embedding space, eliminating the need for a separate embedding model. Trained with a combination of alignment, contrastive, and rank distillation losses, our method retains 97\% of baseline retrieval quality while enabling the LLM agent to search with its own representations. Experiments on the QReCC conversational search benchmark show competitive Recall@10 and MRR@10 compared to the standard generate-then-encode pipeline, with systematic ablations confirming the contribution of each loss component.
title_zh: 一个模型足矣：从大型语言模型代理的隐藏状态中提取原生检索嵌入
abstract_zh: 通常情况下，具备外部知识检索能力的大型语言模型（LLM）代理会先生成一个文本形式的搜索查询，然后使用独立的嵌入模型将其编码为向量。这种两阶段模型架构增加了基础设施复杂性和延迟，却存在冗余：LLM本身已经在其隐藏状态中编码了完整的对话上下文。为此，我们提出通过添加一个轻量级投影头，使LLM代理具备原生检索能力，直接将隐藏状态映射到嵌入空间，从而无需依赖独立的嵌入模型。该方法通过结合对齐损失、对比损失和排序蒸馏损失进行训练，在保持基线检索质量97%的同时，使LLM代理能够利用自身表示进行搜索。在QReCC对话式搜索基准上的实验表明，我们的方法在Recall@10和MRR@10指标上与标准的“生成-再编码”流水线相比具有竞争力，系统性的消融实验也验证了每种损失组件的贡献。

title: IronEngine: Towards General AI Assistant
authors: Xi Mo
abstract: This paper presents IronEngine, a general AI assistant platform organized around a unified orchestration core that connects a desktop user interface, REST and WebSocket APIs, Python clients, local and cloud model backends, persistent memory, task scheduling, reusable skills, 24-category tool execution, MCP-compatible extensibility, and hardware-facing integration. IronEngine introduces a three-phase pipeline -- Discussion (Planner--Reviewer collaboration), Model Switch (VRAM-aware transition), and Execution (tool-augmented action loop) -- that separates planning quality from execution capability. The system features a hierarchical memory architecture with multi-level consolidation, a vectorized skill repository backed by ChromaDB, an adaptive model management layer supporting 92 model profiles with VRAM-aware context budgeting, and an intelligent tool routing system with 130+ alias normalization and automatic error correction. We present experimental results on file operation benchmarks achieving 100\% task completion with a mean total time of 1541 seconds across four heterogeneous tasks, and provide detailed comparisons with representative AI assistant systems including ChatGPT, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, and open-source agent frameworks. Without disclosing proprietary prompts or core algorithms, this paper analyzes the platform's architectural decomposition, subsystem design, experimental performance, safety boundaries, and comparative engineering advantages. The resulting study positions IronEngine as a system-oriented foundation for general-purpose personal assistants, automation frameworks, and future human-centered agent platforms.
title_zh: IronEngine：迈向通用人工智能助手
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了IronEngine——一个以统一编排核心为核心的通用AI助手平台，该平台整合了桌面用户界面、REST与WebSocket API、Python客户端、本地及云端模型后端、持久化内存、任务调度、可复用技能、24类工具执行能力、兼容MCP的可扩展性以及面向硬件的集成。IronEngine引入了一个三阶段流水线：讨论（规划者-评审者协作）、模型切换（VRAM感知的过渡）和执行（工具增强的动作循环），将规划质量与执行能力进行解耦。系统具备分层内存架构，支持多级合并；基于ChromaDB的向量化技能库；支持92种模型配置的自适应模型管理层，具备VRAM感知的上下文预算机制；以及包含130多个别名标准化和自动错误纠正功能的智能工具路由系统。实验结果表明，在文件操作基准测试中，四类异构任务均实现了100%的任务完成率，平均总耗时为1541秒。同时，本文还对ChatGPT、Claude Desktop、Cursor、Windsurf以及开源代理框架等代表性AI助手系统进行了详尽对比。在不披露专有提示词或核心算法的前提下，本文分析了该平台的架构分解、子系统设计、实验性能表现、安全边界以及工程上的比较优势。研究结果表明，IronEngine可作为通用个人助手、自动化框架以及未来以人为中心的智能体平台的系统级基础。

title: A Hierarchical Error-Corrective Graph Framework for Autonomous Agents with LLM-Based Action Generation
authors: Cong Cao, Jingyao Zhang, Kun Tong
abstract: We propose a Hierarchical Error-Corrective Graph FrameworkforAutonomousAgentswithLLM-BasedActionGeneration(HECG),whichincorporates three core innovations: (1) Multi-Dimensional Transferable Strategy (MDTS): by integrating task quality metrics (Q), confidence/cost metrics (C), reward metrics (R), and LLM-based semantic reasoning scores (LLM-Score), MDTS achieves multi-dimensional alignment between quantitative performance and semantic context, enabling more precise selection of high-quality candidate strate gies and effectively reducing the risk of negative transfer. (2) Error Matrix Classification (EMC): unlike simple confusion matrices or overall performance metrics, EMC provides structured attribution of task failures by categorizing errors into ten types, such as Strategy Errors (Strategy Whe) and Script Parsing Errors (Script-Parsing-Error), and decomposing them according to severity, typical actions, error descriptions, and recoverability. This allows precise analysis of the root causes of task failures, offering clear guidance for subsequent error correction and strategy optimization rather than relying solely on overall success rates or single performance metrics. (3) Causal-Context Graph Retrieval (CCGR): to enhance agent retrieval capabilities in dynamic task environments, we construct graphs from historical states, actions, and event sequences, where nodes store executed actions, next-step actions, execution states, transferable strategies, and other relevant information, and edges represent causal dependencies such as preconditions for transitions between nodes. CCGR identifies subgraphs most relevant to the current task context, effectively capturing structural relationships beyond vector similarity, allowing agents to fully leverage contextual information, accelerate strategy adaptation, and improve execution reliability in complex, multi-step tasks.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型动作生成的自主智能体分层纠错图框架
abstract_zh:
我们提出了一种基于大语言模型（LLM）动作生成的自主智能体分层纠错图框架（HECG），该框架包含三项核心创新：

（1）多维可迁移策略（MDTS）：通过整合任务质量指标（Q）、置信度/成本指标（C）、奖励指标（R）以及基于大语言模型的语义推理得分（LLM-Score），MDTS实现了量化性能与语义上下文之间的多维度对齐，从而能够更精准地筛选高质量候选策略，有效降低负面迁移的风险。

（2）错误矩阵分类（EMC）：不同于简单的混淆矩阵或整体性能指标，EMC通过将任务失败系统性地划分为十类错误类型（如策略错误 Strategy-Error 和脚本解析错误 Script-Parsing-Error），并依据严重程度、典型行为、错误描述及可恢复性进行分解，实现对任务失败根源的精确归因分析。这为后续的错误修正与策略优化提供了清晰指导，而非仅依赖总体成功率或单一性能指标。

（3）因果-上下文图检索（CCGR）：为提升智能体在动态任务环境中的检索能力，我们基于历史状态、动作序列和事件流构建图结构，其中节点存储已执行动作、下一步动作、执行状态、可迁移策略等关键信息，边则表示因果依赖关系，如节点间状态转移的前置条件。CCGR能够识别与当前任务上下文最相关的子图，超越传统向量相似性的局限，有效捕捉深层次的结构化关联，使智能体能够充分挖掘上下文信息，加速策略适应过程，并显著提升在复杂多步任务中的执行可靠性。

title: Trust Nothing: RTOS Security without Run-Time Software TCB (Extended Version)
authors: Eric Ackermann, Sven Bugiel
abstract: Embedded devices face an ever-expanding threat landscape: vulnerabilities in application software, operating system kernels, and peripherals threaten the embedded device integrity. Existing computer-architectural defenses fully consider at most two of these threat vectors in their security model. This paper aims at addressing this gap using a novel capability architecture. To this end, we combine a token capability approach suitable for building an untrusted operating system with protection against malicious devices without requiring hardware changes to peripherals. First, we develop and evaluate a full FPGA implementation of our capability architecture around legacy hardware components. Further, we present a soft real-time operating system based on Zephyr that has no run-time software TCB. To this end, we disaggregate Zephyr's subsystems into small, mutually isolated components. All subsystems that exist at run time, including scheduler, allocator and DMA drivers, and all peripherals are fully untrusted. We believe that our work offers a foundation for more rigorous security-by-design in tomorrow's security-critical embedded devices.
title_zh: 不可轻信：无需运行时软件信任根的RTOS安全（扩展版）
abstract_zh: 嵌入式设备面临的威胁环境日益扩大：应用软件、操作系统内核以及外设中的漏洞正威胁着嵌入式设备的完整性。现有的计算机架构防御机制最多仅在安全模型中考虑了其中两个威胁向量。本文旨在通过一种新颖的能力架构来弥补这一差距。为此，我们结合了一种适用于构建不可信操作系统的令牌能力方法，并在无需对周边设备进行硬件修改的情况下，实现了对恶意设备的防护。首先，我们在传统的硬件组件基础上，开发并评估了一个完整的FPGA实现的能力建构。此外，我们提出了一种基于Zephyr的软实时操作系统，其运行时无任何软件可信计算基（TCB）。为此，我们将Zephyr的各个子系统拆分为多个相互隔离的小型组件。所有在运行时存在的子系统，包括调度器、分配器、DMA驱动程序以及所有外设，均被视为完全不可信。我们认为，我们的工作为未来安全关键型嵌入式设备提供了更严格的安全设计基础。

title: Experience on Automatically Converting a C++ Monolith to Java EE
authors: Andre Vehreschild, Lexi Pimenidis
abstract: Converting a large C++ code base (800k lines of code) into Java alone is challenging. Changing the architecture from a monolith into an application adhering to the Java application server standard and to run it on WildFly is a different number. This report describes the experience made during the C++ to Java conversion, the techniques used as well as the way to success of running the Java code on the application server for the first time. The approaches to solve the usual C++ to Java culprits, like multiple inheritance, enum-handling and scoped objects are described. A clang-tool-based software is developed to continuously regenerate the Java, because development on the C++ code base continued.
title_zh: 将C++单体应用自动转换为Java EE的经验
abstract_zh: 将一个庞大的C++代码库（80万行代码）转换为Java本身已极具挑战性。而将架构从单体式系统重构为符合Java应用服务器标准、并能在WildFly上运行的应用程序，则是另一个完全不同的难题。本报告描述了在进行C++到Java转换过程中积累的经验，所采用的技术方法，以及首次成功在应用服务器上运行Java代码的关键步骤。文中还详细介绍了如何解决C++转Java过程中常见的典型问题，例如多重继承、枚举处理以及作用域对象的管理。此外，为应对C++代码库持续开发的情况，我们开发了一款基于clang-tool的软件，用于持续自动生成Java代码。

title: CCR-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating LLMs on Complex Constraints, Control Flows, and Real-World Cases
authors: Xiaona Xue, Yiqiao Huang, Jiacheng Li, Yuanhang Zheng, Huiqi Miao, Yunfei Ma, Rui Liu, Xinbao Sun, Minglu Liu, Fanyu Meng, Chao Deng, Junlan Feng
abstract: Enhancing the ability of large language models (LLMs) to follow complex instructions is critical for their deployment in real-world applications. However, existing evaluation methods often oversimplify instruction complexity as a mere additive combination of atomic constraints, failing to adequately capture the high-dimensional complexity arising from the intricate interplay of content and format, logical workflow control, and real-world applications. This leads to a significant gap between current evaluation practices and practical demands. To bridge this gap, we introduce CCR-Bench, a novel benchmark designed to assess LLMs' adherence to complex instructions. CCR-Bench is characterized by: (1) deep entanglement of content and formatting requirements in task specifications; (2) instructions that involve intricate task decomposition, conditional reasoning, and procedural planning; and (3) evaluation samples derived entirely from real-world industrial scenarios. Extensive experiments on CCR-Bench demonstrate that even state-of-the-art models exhibit substantial performance deficiencies, clearly quantifying the gap between current LLM capabilities and the demands of realworld instruction understanding. We believe that CCR-Bench offers a more rigorous and realistic evaluation framework, advancing the development of LLMs toward the next generation of models capable of understanding and executing complex tasks in industrial applications.
title_zh: CCR-Bench：一个全面的基准测试，用于评估大语言模型在复杂约束、控制流和真实场景中的表现
abstract_zh: 提升大型语言模型（LLMs）理解复杂指令的能力，对于其在真实世界应用中的部署至关重要。然而，现有的评估方法往往将指令复杂性简化为基本约束的简单叠加，未能充分捕捉内容与格式、逻辑流程控制以及现实应用场景之间复杂交互所引发的高维复杂性。这导致当前评估实践与实际需求之间存在显著差距。为弥合这一鸿沟，我们提出了CCR-Bench——一个旨在评估LLMs对复杂指令遵循能力的新基准。CCR-Bench具有以下特点：（1）任务说明中内容要求与格式要求深度交织；（2）指令涉及复杂的任务分解、条件推理和过程规划；（3）评估样本完全来源于真实的工业场景。在CCR-Bench上的大量实验表明，即使最先进的模型也表现出显著的能力不足，清晰地量化了当前LLM能力与真实世界指令理解需求之间的差距。我们认为，CCR-Bench提供了一个更为严格且贴近实际的评估框架，将推动LLMs向下一代能够理解和执行复杂工业任务的模型发展。

title: EvoScientist: Towards Multi-Agent Evolving AI Scientists for End-to-End Scientific Discovery
authors: Yougang Lyu, Xi Zhang, Xinhao Yi, Yuyue Zhao, Shuyu Guo, Wenxiang Hu, Jan Piotrowski, Jakub Kaliski, Jacopo Urbani, Zaiqiao Meng, Lun Zhou, Xiaohui Yan
abstract: The increasing adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) has enabled AI scientists to perform complex end-to-end scientific discovery tasks requiring coordination of specialized roles, including idea generation and experimental execution. However, most state-of-the-art AI scientist systems rely on static, hand-designed pipelines and fail to adapt based on accumulated interaction histories. As a result, these systems overlook promising research directions, repeat failed experiments, and pursue infeasible ideas. To address this, we introduce EvoScientist, an evolving multi-agent AI scientist framework that continuously improves research strategies through persistent memory and self-evolution. EvoScientist comprises three specialized agents: a Researcher Agent (RA) for scientific idea generation, an Engineer Agent (EA) for experiment implementation and execution, and an Evolution Manager Agent (EMA) that distills insights from prior interactions into reusable knowledge. EvoScientist contains two persistent memory modules: (i) an ideation memory, which summarizes feasible research directions from top-ranked ideas while recording previously unsuccessful directions; and (ii) an experimentation memory, which captures effective data processing and model training strategies derived from code search trajectories and best-performing implementations. These modules enable the RA and EA to retrieve relevant prior strategies, improving idea quality and code execution success rates over time. Experiments show that EvoScientist outperforms 7 open-source and commercial state-of-the-art systems in scientific idea generation, achieving higher novelty, feasibility, relevance, and clarity via automatic and human evaluation. EvoScientist also substantially improves code execution success rates through multi-agent evolution, demonstrating persistent memory's effectiveness for end-to-end scientific discovery.
title_zh: EvoScientist：面向多智能体进化的人工智能科学家，实现端到端的科学发现
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的日益普及，使得人工智能科学家能够执行需要协调多种专业角色的复杂端到端科学发现任务，包括创意生成和实验执行。然而，目前大多数最先进的AI科学家系统依赖于静态的手动设计流程，无法根据积累的交互历史进行自适应调整。因此，这些系统往往会忽略有前景的研究方向，重复失败的实验，并追求不切实际的想法。为解决这一问题，我们提出了EvoScientist——一种可演化的多智能体AI科学家框架，通过持续记忆与自我进化机制不断优化研究策略。EvoScientist包含三个专业智能体：负责科学创意生成的科研智能体（RA），负责实验实现与执行的工程师智能体（EA），以及负责从过往交互中提炼洞察并转化为可复用知识的演化管理智能体（EMA）。该框架配备了两个持久化记忆模块：(i) 创意记忆，用于总结排名靠前的可行研究方向，同时记录此前失败的研究路径；(ii) 实验记忆，通过代码搜索轨迹和表现最佳的实现方案，捕获有效的数据处理与模型训练策略。这两个模块使RA和EA能够检索以往的相关策略，从而随着时间推移不断提升创意质量与代码执行成功率。实验结果表明，EvoScientist在科学创意生成方面优于7个开源及商业领域的最先进系统，无论通过自动评估还是人工评价，均展现出更高的新颖性、可行性、相关性和清晰度。此外，EvoScientist还通过多智能体演化显著提升了代码执行的成功率，充分证明了持久化记忆在端到端科学发现中的有效性。

title: DyLLM: Efficient Diffusion LLM Inference via Saliency-based Token Selection and Partial Attention
authors: Younjoo Lee, Junghoo Lee, Seungkyun Dan, Jaiyoung Park, Jung Ho Ahn
abstract: Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) enable parallel token decoding, providing a promising alternative to the sequential nature of autoregressive generation. However, their iterative denoising process remains computationally expensive because it repeatedly processes the entire sequence at every step. We observe that across these diffusion steps, most token representations remain stable; only a small subset, which we term salient tokens, contributes meaningfully to the next update. Leveraging this temporal sparsity, we present DyLLM, a training-free inference framework that accelerates decoding by selectively computing only these salient tokens. DyLLM identifies saliency by measuring the cosine similarity of attention contexts between adjacent denoising steps. It recomputes feed-forward and attention operations only for salient tokens while reusing cached activations for the remainder. Across diverse reasoning and code-generation benchmarks, DyLLM achieves up to 9.6x higher throughput while largely preserving the baseline accuracy of state-of-the-art models like LLaDA and Dream.
title_zh: DyLLM：基于显著性标记选择与部分注意力的高效扩散语言模型推理
abstract_zh: 掩码扩散语言模型（MDLMs）实现了并行的词元解码，为替代自回归生成的串行特性提供了一种有前景的方案。然而，其迭代去噪过程仍存在计算开销大的问题，因为每一步都需要重复处理整个序列。我们观察到，在这些扩散步骤中，大多数词元表示保持稳定；只有少数我们称之为显著词元（salient tokens）的部分对下一步更新有实质性贡献。基于这一时间上的稀疏性，我们提出了 DyLLM——一种无需训练的推理框架，通过仅选择性地计算这些显著词元来加速解码过程。DyLLM 通过测量相邻去噪步骤间注意力上下文的余弦相似度来识别显著性，并仅对显著词元重新计算前馈和注意力操作，而其余部分则复用缓存的激活值。在多种推理与代码生成基准测试中，DyLLM 实现了高达 9.6 倍的吞吐量提升，同时基本保持了 LLaDA 和 Dream 等先进模型的基准准确率。

title: Agentic Critical Training
authors: Weize Liu, Minghui Liu, Sy-Tuyen Ho, Souradip Chakraborty, Xiyao Wang, Furong Huang
abstract: Training large language models (LLMs) as autonomous agents often begins with imitation learning, but it only teaches agents what to do without understanding why: agents never contrast successful actions against suboptimal alternatives and thus lack awareness of action quality. Recent approaches attempt to address this by introducing self-reflection supervision derived from contrasts between expert and alternative actions. However, the training paradigm fundamentally remains imitation learning: the model imitates pre-constructed reflection text rather than learning to reason autonomously. We propose Agentic Critical Training (ACT), a reinforcement learning paradigm that trains agents to identify the better action among alternatives. By rewarding whether the model's judgment is correct, ACT drives the model to autonomously develop reasoning about action quality, producing genuine self-reflection rather than imitating it. Across three challenging agent benchmarks, ACT consistently improves agent performance when combined with different post-training methods. It achieves an average improvement of 5.07 points over imitation learning and 4.62 points over reinforcement learning. Compared to approaches that inject reflection capability through knowledge distillation, ACT also demonstrates clear advantages, yielding an average improvement of 2.42 points. Moreover, ACT enables strong out-of-distribution generalization on agentic benchmarks and improves performance on general reasoning benchmarks without any reasoning-specific training data, highlighting the value of our method. These results suggest that ACT is a promising path toward developing more reflective and capable LLM agents.
title_zh: 代理批判训练
abstract_zh:
训练大型语言模型（LLMs）作为自主智能体时，通常始于模仿学习，但这种方法仅教会智能体“做什么”，而无法理解“为什么”。由于智能体从未对比过成功行为与次优选择，因此缺乏对行为质量的认知。近期的一些方法尝试通过引入基于专家行为与替代行为对比的自我反思监督来解决这一问题。然而，其训练范式本质上仍属于模仿学习：模型只是模仿预先构建的反思文本，而非自主地学习推理能力。

我们提出了一种名为**智能体批判性训练**（Agentic Critical Training, ACT）的强化学习范式，旨在训练智能体能够从多个备选行为中识别出更优者。通过奖励模型判断结果的正确性，ACT促使模型自主发展对行为质量的推理能力，从而生成真正意义上的自我反思，而非简单模仿。

在三个具有挑战性的智能体基准测试中，ACT与不同后训练方法结合使用时，均持续提升了智能体性能。相比模仿学习，ACT平均提升5.07分；相比强化学习，平均提升4.62分。相较于通过知识蒸馏注入反思能力的方法，ACT也展现出明显优势，平均提升达2.42分。此外，ACT在智能体任务的分布外泛化能力上表现强劲，并且在无需任何专门推理训练数据的情况下，显著提升了通用推理基准上的表现，充分体现了该方法的价值。

这些结果表明，ACT为构建更具反思性与能力的LLM智能体提供了一条极具前景的发展路径。

title: SmartGraphical: A Human-in-the-Loop Framework for Detecting Smart Contract Logical Vulnerabilities via Pattern-Driven Static Analysis and Visual Abstraction
authors: Ali Fattahdizaji, Mohammad Pishdar, Zarina Shukur
abstract: Smart contracts are fundamental components of blockchain ecosystems; however, their security remains a critical concern due to inherent vulnerabilities. While existing detection methodologies are predominantly syntax-oriented, targeting reentrancy and arithmetic errors, they often overlook logical flaws arising from defective business logic. This paper introduces SmartGraphical, a novel security framework specifically engineered to identify logical attack surfaces. By synthesizing automated static analysis with an interactive graphical representation of contract architectures, SmartGraphical facilitates a comprehensive inspection of a contract's functional control flow. To mitigate the context-dependent nature of logical bugs, the tool adopts a human-in-the-loop approach, empowering developers to interpret heuristic warnings within a visualized structural context. The efficacy of SmartGraphical was validated through a rigorous empirical evaluation involving a large dataset of real-world contracts and a large-scale user study with 100 developers of varying expertise. Furthermore, the framework's performance was demonstrated through case studies on high-profile exploits, such as the SYFI rebase failure and farming protocol flash swap attacks, proving that SmartGraphical identifies intricate vulnerabilities that elude state-of-the-art automated detectors. Our findings indicate that this hybrid methodology significantly enhances the interpretability and detection rate of non-trivial logical security threats in smart contracts.
title_zh: SmartGraphical：一种通过模式驱动的静态分析与可视化抽象实现人机协同检测智能合约逻辑漏洞的框架
abstract_zh: 智能合约是区块链生态系统中的核心组成部分，但其安全性始终是一个关键问题，主要源于固有的漏洞。尽管现有的检测方法大多以语法为导向，专注于重入攻击和算术错误等常见缺陷，却常常忽视由业务逻辑设计不当引发的逻辑性漏洞。本文提出了一种名为 SmartGraphical 的新型安全框架，专门用于识别逻辑层面的攻击面。该框架通过将自动化静态分析与合同架构的交互式图形化表示相结合，实现了对合约功能控制流的全面审查。为应对逻辑漏洞的高度上下文依赖性，SmartGraphical 采用“人机协同”（human-in-the-loop）机制，使开发者能够在可视化结构背景下理解并评估启发式警告，从而提升判断准确性。通过大规模真实合约数据集的实证评估以及包含100名不同经验水平开发者的广泛用户研究，验证了 SmartGraphical 的有效性。此外，通过对知名漏洞案例（如 SYFI 重置失败事件及农耕协议闪电交换攻击）的深入分析，进一步证明该框架能够发现当前最先进的自动化检测工具难以捕捉的复杂漏洞。研究结果表明，这种混合方法显著提升了对非平凡逻辑安全威胁的可解释性与检测率。

title: RetroAgent: From Solving to Evolving via Retrospective Dual Intrinsic Feedback
authors: Xiaoying Zhang, Zichen Liu, Yipeng Zhang, Xia Hu, Wenqi Shao
abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based agents trained with reinforcement learning (RL) have shown strong potential on complex interactive tasks. However, standard RL paradigms favor static problem-solving over continuous adaptation: agents often converge to suboptimal strategies due to insufficient exploration, while learned knowledge remains implicit within parameters rather than explicitly retrievable, limiting effective experiential learning. To address these limitations, we introduce RetroAgent, an online RL framework that empowers agents to master complex interactive environments not just by solving, but by evolving. Concretely, RetroAgent features a hindsight self-reflection mechanism that produces dual intrinsic feedback: (1) intrinsic numerical feedback that that tracks incremental subtask completion relative to prior attempts, rewarding promising explorations, and (2) intrinsic language feedback that distills reusable lessons into a memory buffer, retrieved via our proposed Similarity & Utility-Aware Upper Confidence Bound (SimUtil-UCB) strategy balancing relevance, utility, and exploration to effectively leverage past experiences. Extensive experiments on two model families across four challenging agentic tasks demonstrate that RetroAgent significantly outperforms existing methods, achieving state-of-the-art results -- e.g., surpassing Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)-trained agents by +18.3% on ALFWorld, +15.4% on WebShop, +27.1% on Sokoban, and +8.9% on MineSweeper -- while exhibiting strong test-time adaptation and generalization to out-of-distribution scenarios.
title_zh: RetroAgent：通过回顾性双重内在反馈实现从求解到演进
abstract_zh: 基于大型语言模型（LLM）的智能体，通过强化学习（RL）训练，在复杂交互任务中展现出巨大潜力。然而，传统的强化学习范式更倾向于静态问题求解而非持续适应：智能体常因探索不足而收敛到次优策略，且所学知识以隐式方式存储于参数之中，难以显式提取，从而限制了有效经验学习的实现。为解决上述局限，我们提出RetroAgent——一种在线强化学习框架，使智能体不仅能解决问题，更能不断进化以掌握复杂的交互环境。具体而言，RetroAgent引入了一种“事后自我反思”机制，生成双重内在反馈：（1）内在数值反馈，用于追踪相对于先前尝试的子任务完成度的增量进展，奖励有前景的探索行为；（2）内在语言反馈，将可复用的经验提炼为记忆缓冲区，并通过我们提出的“相似性与效用感知置信上界”（SimUtil-UCB）策略进行检索，该策略在相关性、实用性与探索之间取得平衡，从而高效利用过往经验。在两大模型家族、四个具有挑战性的代理任务上的大量实验表明，RetroAgent显著优于现有方法，达到当前最优性能——例如，在ALFWorld任务上超越GRPO训练的智能体18.3%，在WebShop任务上提升15.4%，在Sokoban任务上提高27.1%，在MineSweeper任务上增加8.9%；同时展现出强大的测试时自适应能力以及对分布外场景的泛化性能。

title: SCAFFOLD-CEGIS: Preventing Latent Security Degradation in LLM-Driven Iterative Code Refinement
authors: Yi Chen, Yun Bian, Haiquan Wang, Shihao Li, Zhe Cui
abstract: The application of large language models to code generation has evolved from one-shot generation to iterative refinement, yet the evolution of security throughout iteration remains insufficiently understood. Through comparative experiments on three mainstream LLMs, this paper reveals the iterative refinement paradox: specification drift during multi-objective optimization causes security to degrade gradually over successive iterations. Taking GPT-4o as an example, 43.7 % of iteration chains contain more vulnerabilities than the baseline after ten rounds, and cross-model experiments show that this phenomenon is prevalent. Further analysis shows that simply introducing static application security testing (SAST) gating cannot effectively suppress degradation; instead, it increases the latent security degradation rate from 12.5% under the unprotected baseline to 20.8 %. The root cause is that static-analysis rules cannot cover structural degradations such as the removal of defensive logic or the weakening of exception handling. To address this problem, we propose the SCAFFOLD-CEGIS framework. Drawing on the counterexample-guided inductive synthesis (CEGIS) paradigm, the framework adopts a multi-agent collaborative architecture that transforms security constraints from implicit prompts into explicit verifiable constraints. It automatically identifies and solidifies security-critical elements as hard constraints through semantic anchoring, enforces safety monotonicity through four-layer gated verification, and continuously assimilates experience from failures. Comparative experiments against six existing defense methods show that the full framework reduces the latent security degradation rate to 2.1% and achieves a safety monotonicity rate of 100%.
title_zh: SCAFFOLD-CEGIS：防止大语言模型驱动的迭代代码优化中的潜在安全退化
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型在代码生成中的应用已从一次性生成演进至迭代优化，然而在迭代过程中安全性的演变仍缺乏充分理解。通过对三种主流大语言模型的对比实验，本文揭示了迭代优化中的悖论：在多目标优化过程中，规范漂移导致安全性随迭代轮次逐步退化。以GPT-4o为例，在经过十轮迭代后，43.7%的生成链路所含漏洞数量超过基线水平；跨模型实验进一步表明，该现象普遍存在。深入分析发现，仅引入静态应用安全测试（SAST）作为准入门槛，并不能有效抑制安全性的退化，反而使潜在的安全退化率从无防护基线下的12.5%上升至20.8%。其根本原因在于，静态分析规则无法覆盖诸如防御逻辑被移除或异常处理机制弱化等结构性退化问题。

为解决这一难题，本文提出SCAFFOLD-CEGIS框架。该框架借鉴反例引导归纳合成（CEGIS）范式，采用多智能体协同架构，将原本隐含于提示中的安全约束转化为可显式验证的约束条件。通过语义锚定技术，自动识别并固化安全关键元素为硬性约束，借助四层门控验证机制确保安全性的单调性，并持续吸收失败经验以实现自我进化。与六种现有防御方法的对比实验表明，该完整框架将潜在安全退化率降低至2.1%，并实现了100%的安全性单调性。

title: SPD-RAG: Sub-Agent Per Document Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Yagiz Can Akay, Muhammed Yusuf Kartal, Esra Alparslan, Faruk Ortakoyluoglu, Arda Akpinar
abstract: Answering complex, real-world queries often requires synthesizing facts scattered across vast document corpora. In these settings, standard retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines suffer from incomplete evidence coverage, while long-context large language models (LLMs) struggle to reason reliably over massive inputs. We introduce SPD-RAG, a hierarchical multi-agent framework for exhaustive cross-document question answering that decomposes the problem along the document axis. Each document is processed by a dedicated document-level agent operating only on its own content, enabling focused retrieval, while a coordinator dispatches tasks to relevant agents and aggregates their partial answers. Agent outputs are synthesized by merging partial answers through a token-bounded synthesis layer (which supports recursive map-reduce for massive corpora). This document-level specialization with centralized fusion improves scalability and answer quality in heterogeneous multidocument settings while yielding a modular, extensible retrieval pipeline. On the LOONG benchmark (EMNLP 2024) for long-context multi-document QA, SPD-RAG achieves an Avg Score of 58.1 (GPT-5 evaluation), outperforming Normal RAG (33.0) and Agentic RAG (32.8) while using only 38% of the API cost of a full-context baseline (68.0).
title_zh: SPD-RAG：按文档子代理检索增强生成
abstract_zh: 回答复杂的现实世界问题通常需要整合散布在庞大文档集合中的各种事实。在这一背景下，传统的检索增强生成（RAG）流程往往因证据覆盖不全而受限，而长上下文大语言模型（LLM）则难以在海量输入上进行可靠推理。为此，我们提出了SPD-RAG——一种分层多智能体框架，用于实现跨文档问答的全面覆盖，其通过文档维度对问题进行分解。每个文档由一个专门的文档级智能体处理，该智能体仅基于自身内容进行操作，从而实现聚焦式检索；同时，一个协调器负责将任务分配给相关智能体，并聚合它们的部分答案。各智能体的输出通过一个具有 token 限制的合成层进行融合（支持针对大规模语料库的递归 map-reduce 操作）。这种以文档为单位的专业化处理结合集中式融合机制，在异构多文档场景下显著提升了可扩展性与答案质量，同时构建了一个模块化、可扩展的检索管道。在针对长上下文多文档问答的 LOONG 基准测试（EMNLP 2024）中，SPD-RAG 在 GPT-5 评估下取得了 58.1 的平均得分，优于普通 RAG（33.0）和代理式 RAG（32.8），且仅消耗全上下文基线（68.0 分）38% 的 API 成本。

title: Not All Queries Need Deep Thought: CoFiCot for Adaptive Coarse-to-fine Stateful Refinement
authors: Dongxu Zhang, Hongqiang Lin, Yiding Sun, Pengyu Wang, Qirui Wang, Ning Yang, Jihua Zhu
abstract: Scaling test-time computation enhances LLM reasoning ability but faces a uniform computation paradox. Allocating identical resources leads to over-correction on simple tasks and insufficient refinement on complex ones. To address this, we propose CoFiCot, a coarse-to-fine adaptive framework that dynamically tailors inference strategies to problem difficulty. Specifically, we implement a multi-metric classifier that triages queries by synthesizing semantic entropy, consensus reliability, and predicted reasoning depth . This enables a differentiated refinement stage that applies efficient aggregation for simple queries while routing complex ones to a context-aware correction loop . We formalize correction as a stateful sequential propagation process , where each repair is strictly conditioned on the verified history of prior rectifications. By integrating Process Reward Models (PRMs) within this state-dependent trajectory, CoFiCot effectively bridges the gap between granular error localization and global logical coherence, preventing the context fragmentation typical of stateless refinement methods.
title_zh: 并非所有查询都需要深度思考：用于自适应粗到精状态化优化的CoFiCot
abstract_zh: 扩展推理时的计算量能够提升大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力，但同时也面临“资源分配均质化”的悖论：对所有任务分配相同的计算资源，会导致简单任务过度修正，而复杂任务则得不到充分优化。为解决这一问题，我们提出 CoFiCot——一种由粗到精的自适应框架，能够根据问题难度动态调整推理策略。具体而言，我们设计了一个多指标分类器，通过综合语义熵、共识可靠性以及预测推理深度等维度对查询进行分诊。这使得系统能够在不同层次上实施精细化的修正：对于简单查询，采用高效的聚合策略；而对于复杂查询，则将其引导至一个具备上下文感知能力的纠错循环中。我们形式化地将纠错过程建模为一种有状态的序列传播机制，其中每一次修复都严格依赖于先前修正结果的已验证历史。通过在这一状态相关的推理轨迹中集成过程奖励模型（Process Reward Models, PRMs），CoFiCot 有效弥合了细粒度错误定位与全局逻辑一致性之间的鸿沟，避免了传统无状态修正方法常见的上下文碎片化问题。

title: AutoAdapt: An Automated Domain Adaptation Framework for LLMs
authors: Sidharth Sinha, Anson Bastos, Xuchao Zhang, Akshay Nambi, Chetan Bansal, Saravan Rajmohan
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) excel in open domains but struggle in specialized settings with limited data and evolving knowledge. Existing domain adaptation practices rely heavily on manual trial-and-error processes, incur significant hyperparameter complexity, and are highly sensitive to data and user preferences, all under the high cost of LLM training. Moreover, the interactions and transferability of hyperparameter choices across models/domains remain poorly understood, making adaptation gains uncertain even with substantial effort. To solve these challenges, we present AutoAdapt, a novel end-to-end automated framework for efficient and reliable LLM domain adaptation. AutoAdapt leverages curated knowledge bases from literature and open-source resources to reduce expert intervention. To narrow the search space, we design a novel multi-agent debating system in which proposal and critic agents iteratively interact to align user intent and incorporate data signals and best practices into the planning process. To optimize hyperparameters under tight budgets, we propose AutoRefine, a novel LLM-based surrogate that replaces costly black-box search. Across 10 tasks, AutoAdapt achieves a 25% average relative accuracy improvement over state-of-the-art Automated Machine Learning baselines with minimal overhead.
title_zh: AutoAdapt：一种面向大语言模型的自动化领域自适应框架
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在开放领域表现出色，但在数据有限且知识持续演化的专业场景中却面临挑战。现有的领域适应方法严重依赖人工试错，存在显著的超参数复杂性，对数据和用户偏好高度敏感，且 LLM 训练成本高昂。此外，超参数选择在不同模型或领域间的交互关系与可迁移性仍不明确，即使投入大量精力，适应效果也难以保证。为解决上述问题，我们提出 AutoAdapt——一种新颖的端到端自动化框架，旨在实现高效、可靠的 LLM 领域适应。AutoAdapt 利用来自文献和开源资源的精选知识库，减少对专家干预的需求。为缩小搜索空间，我们设计了一种创新的多智能体辩论系统，其中提议智能体与批评智能体迭代互动，将用户意图、数据信号及最佳实践融入规划过程。为在严格预算下优化超参数，我们提出 AutoRefine，一种基于 LLM 的新型代理模型，替代昂贵的黑箱搜索。在 10 项任务上的实验表明，AutoAdapt 相较于当前最先进的自动化机器学习基线，平均相对准确率提升达 25%，且开销极小。

title: An explainable hybrid deep learning-enabled intelligent fault detection and diagnosis approach for automotive software systems validation
authors: Mohammad Abboush, Ehab Ghannoum, Andreas Rausch
abstract: Advancements in data-driven machine learning have emerged as a pivotal element in supporting automotive software systems (ASSs) engineering across various levels of the V-development process. Duringsystemverificationandvalidation,theintegrationofanintelligent fault detection anddiagnosis (FDD) model with test recordings analysis process serves as a powerful tool for efficiency ensuring functional safety. However, the lack of interpretability of the black-box FDD models developed not only hinders understanding of the cause underlying the prediction, but also prevents the model from being adapted based on the prediction result. This, in turn, increases the computational cost required for developingacomplexFDDmodelandlimitsconfidenceinreal-timesafety-criticalapplications.To address this challenge, a novel explainable method for fault detection, identification, and localization is proposed in this article with the aim of providing a clear understanding of the logic behind the prediction outcome. To this end, a hybrid 1dCNN-GRU-based intelligent model was developed to analyze the recordings from the real-time validation process of ASSs. The employment of explainable AI techniques, i.e., IGs, DeepLIFT, Gradient SHAP, and DeepLIFT SHAP, was instrumental in enabling model adaptation and facilitating the root cause analysis (RCA). The proposed approach is applied to the real time dataset collected during a virtual test drive performed by the user on hardware in the loop system.
title_zh: 一种可解释的混合深度学习驱动的智能故障检测与诊断方法，用于汽车软件系统验证
abstract_zh: 数据驱动的机器学习技术的进步已成为支持汽车软件系统（ASSs）工程在V型开发流程各个阶段的关键要素。在系统验证与确认过程中，将智能故障检测与诊断（FDD）模型与测试记录分析过程相结合，成为保障功能安全、提升效率的强大工具。然而，当前所开发的黑箱式FDD模型缺乏可解释性，不仅阻碍了对预测结果背后原因的理解，也使得无法根据预测结果对模型进行调整优化。这反过来增加了复杂FDD模型开发所需的计算成本，并限制了其在实时安全关键应用中的可信度。为应对这一挑战，本文提出了一种新型的可解释故障检测、识别与定位方法，旨在清晰揭示预测结果背后的逻辑依据。为此，我们构建了一种基于1D CNN-GRU的混合智能模型，用于分析ASSs实时验证过程中的测试记录。通过引入可解释人工智能技术——包括梯度归因（IGs）、DeepLIFT、Gradient SHAP以及DeepLIFT SHAP，有效实现了模型的自适应调整，并促进了根本原因分析（RCA）。该方法被应用于用户在硬件在环系统上进行虚拟试驾时采集的真实时间数据集。

title: More to Extract: Discovering MEV by Token Contract Analysis
authors: Jiaqi Chen, Yuzhe Tang, Yue Duan
abstract: This paper tackles the discovery of tMEV, that is, the Maximal Extractable Value on blockchains that arises from Token smart contracts. This scope differs from the existing MEV-discovery research, which analyzes application-layer contracts or attacker contracts, but ignores the wide and diverse range of token contracts. This paper presents a pipeline of techniques for tMEV discovery, including tSCAN, a static analysis tool for identifying non-standard supply-control functions in token contracts, and tSEARCH, a searcher that uncovers profitable tMEV opportunities by generating, refining, and solving token-specific constraints. By replaying real-world transactions, this paper demonstrates both the profitability of tMEV strategies and existing searchers' unawareness of them: the proposed tSEARCH extracts $10\times$ more profit than observed MEV activity on Ethereum. The practicality of tMEV searching is demonstrated through a prototype built on Slither, showing high effectiveness with low performance overhead.
title_zh: 更多可提取内容：通过代币合约分析探索MEV
abstract_zh: 本文致力于发现tMEV，即由代币智能合约引发的区块链上最大可提取价值（Maximal Extractable Value）。这一研究范围不同于现有的MEV发现研究——后者主要分析应用层合约或攻击者合约，却忽视了广泛且多样的代币合约。本文提出了一套用于tMEV发现的技术流程，包括tSCAN，一种用于识别代币合约中非标准供应控制函数的静态分析工具，以及tSEARCH，一种通过生成、优化和求解特定于代币的约束条件来挖掘高利润tMEV机会的搜索器。通过重放真实世界中的交易，本文证明了tMEV策略的盈利能力，以及现有搜索器对这些机会的忽视：所提出的tSEARCH所提取的利润是Ethereum上观察到的MEV活动的10倍。通过基于Slither构建的原型系统，本文还展示了tMEV搜索的实际可行性，该系统在性能开销极低的情况下表现出极高的有效性。

title: ConnChecker: Automated Root-Cause Analysis for Formal Connectivity Check via Graph
authors: Do Ngoc Tiep, Nguyen Linh Anh, Luu Danh Minh
abstract: Formal connectivity checking offers scalable verification of signal paths in complex SoC designs, but debugging counterexamples remains a manual and time-consuming process. ConnChecker introduces a new graph-based perspective for automating root-cause analysis by integrating formal tool outputs such as structural/functional dependency graphs and counterexamples report. It begins with automatic failure categorization, routing each counterexample to one of three targeted analysis flows. These flows localize failure points and suggest corrective actions or hints for manual inspection. Evaluated on two industrial SoCs, ConnChecker achieved up to 80\% reduction in debugging time, especially for complex cases, demonstrating its scalability and effectiveness across diverse connectivity scenarios.
title_zh: ConnChecker：基于图的自动化根因分析方法，用于形式化连通性检查
abstract_zh: 形式化连接性检查为复杂SoC设计中的信号路径验证提供了可扩展的解决方案，但调试反例仍是一个手动且耗时的过程。ConnChecker通过引入基于图的新视角，实现了根因分析的自动化，该方法整合了形式化工具输出的结构/功能依赖图以及反例报告。它首先进行自动故障分类，并将每个反例路由至三个针对性的分析流程之一。这些流程能够精确定位故障点，并为人工检查提供修正建议或提示。在两个工业级SoC上的评估表明，ConnChecker将调试时间最多减少了80%，尤其在复杂场景下表现突出，充分证明了其在多种连接性场景下的可扩展性和有效性。

title: Semantic Risk Scoring of Aggregated Metrics: An AI-Driven Approach for Healthcare Data Governance
authors: Mohammed Omer Shakeel Ahmed
abstract: Large healthcare institutions typically operate multiple business intelligence (BI) teams segmented by domain, including clinical performance, fundraising, operations, and compliance. Due to HIPAA, FERPA, and IRB restrictions, these teams face challenges in sharing patient-level data needed for analytics. To mitigate this, A metric aggregation table is proposed, which is a precomputed, privacy-compliant summary. These abstractions enable decision-making without direct access to sensitive data. However, even aggregated metrics can inadvertently lead to privacy risks if constructed without rigorous safeguards. A modular AI framework is proposed that evaluates SQL-based metric definitions for potential overexposure using both semantic and syntactic features. Specifically, the system parses SQL queries into abstract syntax trees (ASTs), extracts sensitive patterns (e.g., fine-grained GROUP BY on ZIP code or gender), and encodes the logic using pretrained CodeBERT embeddings. These are fused with structural features and passed to an XGBoost classifier trained to assign risk scores. Queries that surpass the risk threshold (e.g., > 0.85) are flagged and returned with human-readable explanations. This enables proactive governance, preventing statistical disclosure before deployment. This implementation demonstrates strong potential for cross-departmental metric sharing in healthcare while maintaining compliance and auditability. The system also promotes role-based access control (RBAC), supports zero-trust data architectures, and aligns with national data modernization goals by ensuring that metric pipelines are explainable, privacy-preserving, and AI-auditable by design. Unlike prior works that rely on runtime data access to flag privacy violations, the proposed framework performs static, explainable detection at the query-level, enabling pre-execution protection and audit readiness
title_zh: 聚合指标的语义风险评分：一种面向医疗数据治理的AI驱动方法
abstract_zh:
大型医疗保健机构通常会按领域划分多个业务智能（BI）团队，包括临床绩效、筹款、运营和合规等。由于受到HIPAA、FERPA以及IRB等法规的限制，这些团队在进行数据分析时难以共享需要的患者级数据。为缓解这一问题，本文提出一种度量聚合表（metric aggregation table），即预先计算且符合隐私保护要求的汇总数据。这种抽象方式使决策无需直接访问敏感数据即可实现。然而，即使经过聚合的指标，若在构建过程中缺乏严格的防护措施，仍可能无意中引发隐私风险。

为此，本文提出一种模块化AI框架，通过结合语义与语法特征，评估基于SQL的度量定义是否存在过度暴露的风险。具体而言，系统将SQL查询解析为抽象语法树（AST），提取敏感模式（如按邮政编码或性别进行细粒度分组），并利用预训练的CodeBERT嵌入对逻辑进行编码。随后，将这些嵌入与结构化特征融合，并输入一个经训练的XGBoost分类器，以生成风险评分。对于风险评分超过阈值（例如 > 0.85）的查询，系统将予以标记，并返回可读性强的人类解释说明。该机制实现了主动治理，可在部署前预防统计信息泄露。

本方案展示了在保障合规性与可审计性的前提下，实现跨部门度量共享的巨大潜力。系统还支持基于角色的访问控制（RBAC），契合零信任数据架构理念，并与国家数据现代化目标保持一致——确保度量管道从设计之初就具备可解释性、隐私保护性和AI可审计性。与以往依赖运行时数据访问来识别隐私违规的方法不同，本框架采用静态、可解释的查询级检测，在查询执行前即完成风险识别，从而实现事前防护与审计就绪。

title: Ares: Adaptive Reasoning Effort Selection for Efficient LLM Agents
authors: Jingbo Yang, Bairu Hou, Wei Wei, Yujia Bao, Shiyu Chang
abstract: Modern agents powered by thinking LLMs achieve high accuracy through long chain-of-thought reasoning but incur substantial inference costs. While many LLMs now support configurable reasoning levels (e.g., high/medium/low), static strategies are often ineffective: using low-effort modes at every step leads to significant performance degradation, while random selection fails to preserve accuracy or provide meaningful cost reduction. However, agents should reserve high reasoning effort for difficult steps like navigating complex website structures, while using lower-effort modes for simpler steps like opening a target URL. In this paper, we propose Ares, a framework for per-step dynamic reasoning effort selection tailored for multi-step agent tasks. Ares employs a lightweight router to predict the lowest appropriate reasoning level for each step based on the interaction history. To train this router, we develop a data generation pipeline that identifies the minimum reasoning effort required for successful step completion. We then fine-tune the router to predict these levels, enabling plug-and-play integration for any LLM agents. We evaluate Ares on a diverse set of agent tasks, including TAU-Bench for tool use agents, BrowseComp-Plus for deep-research agents, and WebArena for web agents. Experimental results show that Ares reduces reasoning token usage by up to 52.7% compared to fixed high-effort reasoning, while introducing minimal degradation in task success rates.
title_zh: 阿瑞斯：用于高效大语言模型代理的自适应推理努力选择
abstract_zh: 由思维型大语言模型（LLM）驱动的现代智能体通过长链式思维推理实现了高精度，但带来了巨大的推理开销。尽管目前许多LLM已支持可配置的推理层级（如高/中/低），但采用静态策略往往效果不佳：在每一步都使用低努力模式会导致性能显著下降；而随机选择则无法保持准确性，也难以实现有意义的成本降低。实际上，智能体应在处理复杂任务步骤时（如导航复杂的网站结构）保留高推理努力，而在执行简单步骤（如打开目标网址）时则使用较低努力模式。本文提出Ares，一个针对多步智能体任务设计的、基于每一步动态调整推理努力水平的框架。Ares采用一个轻量级路由模块，根据交互历史预测每一步所需的最低合适推理层级。为训练该路由模块，我们构建了一套数据生成流水线，用于识别成功完成每一步所需的最小推理努力。随后，我们对路由模块进行微调，使其能够准确预测这些层级，从而实现与任意LLM智能体的即插即用集成。我们在多种智能体任务上评估了Ares的表现，包括用于工具使用智能体的TAU-Bench、用于深度研究智能体的BrowseComp-Plus，以及用于网页智能体的WebArena。实验结果表明，相较于固定高努力推理，Ares将推理Token消耗降低了高达52.7%，同时仅带来极小的任务成功率下降。

title: LeJOT-AutoML: LLM-Driven Feature Engineering for Job Execution Time Prediction in Databricks Cost Optimization
authors: Lizhi Ma, Yi-Xiang Hu, Yihui Ren, Feng Wu, Xiang-Yang Li
abstract: Databricks job orchestration systems (e.g., LeJOT) reduce cloud costs by selecting low-priced compute configurations while meeting latency and dependency constraints. Accurate execution-time prediction under heterogeneous instance types and non-stationary runtime conditions is therefore critical. Existing pipelines rely on static, manually engineered features that under-capture runtime effects (e.g., partition pruning, data skew, and shuffle amplification), and predictive signals are scattered across logs, metadata, and job scripts-lengthening update cycles and increasing engineering overhead. We present LeJOT-AutoML, an agent-driven AutoML framework that embeds large language model agents throughout the ML lifecycle. LeJOT-AutoML combines retrieval-augmented generation over a domain knowledge base with a Model Context Protocol toolchain (log parsers, metadata queries, and a read-only SQL sandbox) to analyze job artifacts, synthesize and validate feature-extraction code via safety gates, and train/select predictors. This design materializes runtime-derived features that are difficult to obtain through static analysis alone. On enterprise Databricks workloads, LeJOT-AutoML generates over 200 features and reduces the feature-engineering and evaluation loop from weeks to 20-30 minutes, while maintaining competitive prediction accuracy. Integrated into the LeJOT pipeline, it enables automated continuous model updates and achieves 19.01% cost savings in our deployment setting through improved orchestration.
title_zh: LeJOT-AutoML：基于大模型的特征工程在Databricks成本优化中的作业执行时间预测
abstract_zh: Databricks 作业编排系统（如 LeJOT）通过选择价格较低的计算资源配置，在满足延迟和依赖约束的前提下，有效降低了云成本。因此，在异构实例类型和非平稳运行时条件下实现准确的执行时间预测至关重要。现有的流水线依赖于静态的手动特征工程，难以充分捕捉运行时效应（如分区裁剪、数据倾斜和洗牌放大），而预测信号则分散在日志、元数据和作业脚本中，导致更新周期延长并增加了工程开销。我们提出了 LeJOT-AutoML，一种由智能体驱动的自动化机器学习框架，将大型语言模型智能体贯穿于整个机器学习生命周期。LeJOT-AutoML 结合领域知识库的检索增强生成技术，以及一套 Model Context Protocol 工具链（包括日志解析器、元数据查询工具和只读 SQL沙箱），用于分析作业产物，通过安全机制合成并验证特征提取代码，进而训练和选择预测模型。该设计能够生成仅靠静态分析难以获取的运行时衍生特征。在企业级 Databricks 工作负载上，LeJOT-AutoML 可生成超过 200 个特征，将特征工程与评估循环从数周缩短至 20-30 分钟，同时保持了具有竞争力的预测精度。集成到 LeJOT 流水线后，该系统实现了自动化的持续模型更新，并在我们的部署环境中通过优化编排实现了 19.01% 的成本节约。

title: In-Context Reinforcement Learning for Tool Use in Large Language Models
authors: Yaoqi Ye, Yiran Zhao, Keyu Duan, Zeyu Zheng, Kenji Kawaguchi, Cihang Xie, Michael Qizhe Shieh
abstract: While large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong reasoning abilities, their performance on complex tasks is often constrained by the limitations of their internal knowledge. A compelling approach to overcome this challenge is to augment these models with external tools -- such as Python interpreters for mathematical computations or search engines for retrieving factual information. However, enabling models to use these tools effectively remains a significant challenge. Existing methods typically rely on cold-start pipelines that begin with supervised fine-tuning (SFT), followed by reinforcement learning (RL). These approaches often require substantial amounts of labeled data for SFT, which is expensive to annotate or synthesize. In this work, we propose In-Context Reinforcement Learning (ICRL), an RL-only framework that eliminates the need for SFT by leveraging few-shot prompting during the rollout stage of RL. Specifically, ICRL introduces in-context examples within the rollout prompts to teach the model how to invoke external tools. Furthermore, as training progresses, the number of in-context examples is gradually reduced, eventually reaching a zero-shot setting where the model learns to call tools independently. We conduct extensive experiments across a range of reasoning and tool-use benchmarks. Results show that ICRL achieves state-of-the-art performance, demonstrating its effectiveness as a scalable, data-efficient alternative to traditional SFT-based pipelines.
title_zh: 大语言模型中用于工具使用的上下文强化学习
abstract_zh: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）展现出强大的推理能力，但其在复杂任务上的表现往往受限于内部知识的不足。一种有前景的解决方案是通过引入外部工具来增强这些模型——例如使用Python解释器进行数学计算，或借助搜索引擎获取事实信息。然而，如何让模型有效利用这些工具仍是一个重大挑战。现有的方法通常依赖于冷启动式流程：先进行监督微调（SFT），再进行强化学习（RL）。这类方法通常需要大量标注数据用于SFT，而这些数据的标注或生成成本高昂。在本研究中，我们提出了一种仅基于强化学习的框架——上下文内强化学习（In-Context Reinforcement Learning, ICRL），该框架无需监督微调，而是通过在强化学习的推演阶段采用少样本提示（few-shot prompting）来实现。具体而言，ICRL在推演提示中引入上下文示例，指导模型如何调用外部工具。随着训练的推进，上下文示例的数量逐步减少，最终达到零样本（zero-shot）设置，使模型能够自主学会调用工具。我们在多个推理与工具使用基准测试上进行了广泛实验。结果表明，ICRL达到了当前最优性能，充分证明了其作为一种可扩展、数据高效替代传统SFT驱动流水线的有效性。

title: Advancing Automated Algorithm Design via Evolutionary Stagewise Design with LLMs
authors: Chen Lu, Ke Xue, Chengrui Gao, Yunqi Shi, Siyuan Xu, Mingxuan Yuan, Chao Qian, Zhi-Hua Zhou
abstract: With the rapid advancement of human science and technology, problems in industrial scenarios are becoming increasingly challenging, bringing significant challenges to traditional algorithm design. Automated algorithm design with LLMs emerges as a promising solution, but the currently adopted black-box modeling deprives LLMs of any awareness of the intrinsic mechanism of the target problem, leading to hallucinated designs. In this paper, we introduce Evolutionary Stagewise Algorithm Design (EvoStage), a novel evolutionary paradigm that bridges the gap between the rigorous demands of industrial-scale algorithm design and the LLM-based algorithm design methods. Drawing inspiration from CoT, EvoStage decomposes the algorithm design process into sequential, manageable stages and integrates real-time intermediate feedback to iteratively refine algorithm design directions. To further reduce the algorithm design space and avoid falling into local optima, we introduce a multi-agent system and a "global-local perspective" mechanism. We apply EvoStage to the design of two types of common optimizers: designing parameter configuration schedules of the Adam optimizer for chip placement, and designing acquisition functions of Bayesian optimization for black-box optimization. Experimental results across open-source benchmarks demonstrate that EvoStage outperforms human-expert designs and existing LLM-based methods within only a couple of evolution steps, even achieving the historically state-of-the-art half-perimeter wire-length results on every tested chip case. Furthermore, when deployed on a commercial-grade 3D chip placement tool, EvoStage significantly surpasses the original performance metrics, achieving record-breaking efficiency. We hope EvoStage can significantly advance automated algorithm design in the real world, helping elevate human productivity.
title_zh: 通过大语言模型实现进化分阶段设计以推进自动化算法设计
abstract_zh: 随着人类科学技术的飞速发展，工业场景中的问题日益复杂，给传统算法设计带来了巨大挑战。基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动化算法设计展现出广阔前景，但当前普遍采用的“黑箱建模”方式使LLM缺乏对目标问题内在机制的理解，导致生成结果出现幻觉性设计。本文提出一种新型进化范式——进化分阶段算法设计（EvoStage），有效弥合了工业级算法设计对严谨性的高要求与基于LLM的算法设计方法之间的鸿沟。受思维链（CoT）启发，EvoStage将算法设计过程分解为一系列可管理的阶段性步骤，并引入实时中间反馈，迭代优化算法设计方向。为进一步缩小算法设计空间、避免陷入局部最优，我们设计了多智能体系统与“全局-局部视角”机制。我们将EvoStage应用于两类常见优化器的设计：一是为芯片布局优化设计Adam优化器的参数配置调度策略；二是为黑箱优化设计贝叶斯优化的采集函数。在多个开源基准上的实验结果表明，EvoStage仅经过数轮进化迭代，便超越了人工专家设计及现有基于LLM的方法，甚至在所有测试芯片案例中均达到了历史最佳的半周长布线长度表现。此外，在部署至商用级3D芯片布局工具时，EvoStage显著优于原始性能指标，创造了新的效率纪录。我们期望EvoStage能切实推动真实世界中自动化算法设计的发展，助力提升人类生产力。

title: Coverage-Guided Multi-Agent Harness Generation for Java Library Fuzzing
authors: Nils Loose, Nico Winkel, Kristoffer Hempel, Felix Mächtle, Julian Hans, Thomas Eisenbarth
abstract: Coverage-guided fuzzing has proven effective for software testing, but targeting library code requires specialized fuzz harnesses that translate fuzzer-generated inputs into valid API invocations. Manual harness creation is time-consuming and requires deep understanding of API semantics, initialization sequences, and exception handling contracts. We present a multi-agent architecture that automates fuzz harness generation for Java libraries through specialized LLM-powered agents. Five ReAct agents decompose the workflow into research, synthesis, compilation repair, coverage analysis, and refinement. Rather than preprocessing entire codebases, agents query documentation, source code, and callgraph information on demand through the Model Context Protocol, maintaining focused context while exploring complex dependencies. To enable effective refinement, we introduce method-targeted coverage that tracks coverage only during target method execution to isolate target behavior, and agent-guided termination that examines uncovered source code to distinguish productive refinement opportunities from diminishing returns. We evaluated our approach on seven target methods from six widely-deployed Java libraries totaling 115,000+ Maven dependents. Our generated harnesses achieve a median 26\% improvement over OSS-Fuzz baselines and outperform Jazzer AutoFuzz by 5\% in package-scope coverage. Generation costs average \$3.20 and 10 minutes per harness, making the approach practical for continuous fuzzing workflows. During a 12-hour fuzzing campaign, our generated harnesses discovered 3 bugs in projects that are already integrated into OSS-Fuzz, demonstrating the effectiveness of the generated harnesses.
title_zh: 面向Java库模糊测试的覆盖引导型多智能体测试用例生成
abstract_zh: 覆盖引导的模糊测试在软件测试中已被证明非常有效，但针对库代码时，需要专门的模糊测试桩（fuzz harnesses）将模糊器生成的输入转换为有效的API调用。手动创建这些桩不仅耗时，还要求对API语义、初始化序列以及异常处理契约有深入理解。本文提出一种多智能体架构，通过专用的大语言模型（LLM）驱动的智能体，自动化生成Java库的模糊测试桩。五个基于ReAct框架的智能体将整个流程分解为研究、合成、编译修复、覆盖率分析和优化改进五个阶段。与预先处理整个代码库不同，这些智能体通过“模型上下文协议”按需查询文档、源代码和调用图信息，在探索复杂依赖关系的同时保持上下文聚焦。为了实现高效的优化，我们引入了方法目标覆盖率机制，仅在目标方法执行期间追踪覆盖率，从而隔离出目标行为；同时提出由智能体引导的终止策略，通过分析未覆盖的源代码来区分具有潜力的优化机会与边际效益递减的情况。我们在六个广泛部署的Java库中的七个目标方法上评估了该方法，这些库合计拥有超过11.5万个Maven依赖项。实验结果表明，所生成的测试桩在中位数上比OSS-Fuzz基线提升了26%，在包级覆盖率方面比Jazzer AutoFuzz高出5%。每条测试桩的生成成本平均仅为3.20美元和10分钟，使其适用于持续模糊测试工作流。在一次12小时的模糊测试活动中，这些生成的测试桩成功发现了已集成到OSS-Fuzz中的项目中的三个漏洞，充分验证了其有效性。

title: Practical Type Inference: High-Throughput Recovery of Real-World Structures and Function Signatures
authors: Lukas Seidel, Sam Thomas, Konrad Rieck
abstract: The recovery of types from stripped binaries is a key to exact decompilation, yet its practical realization suffers. For composite structures in particular, both layout and semantic fidelity are required to enable end-to-end reconstruction. Many existing approaches either synthesize layouts or infer names post-hoc, which weakens downstream usability. This is further aggravated by an excessive runtime overhead that is especially prohibitive in automated environments. We present XTRIDE, an improved n-gram-based approach that focuses on practicality: highly optimized throughput and actionable confidence scores allow for deployment in automated pipelines. When compared to the state of the art in struct recovery, our method achieves comparable performance while being between 70 and 2300 times faster. As our inference is grounded in real-world types, we achieve the highest ratio of fully-correct struct layouts. With an optimized training regimen, our model outperforms the current state of the art on the DIRT dataset by 5.09 percentage points, achieving 90.15% type inference accuracy overall. Furthermore, we show that n-gram-based type prediction generalizes to function signature recovery: conducting a case study on embedded firmware, we show that this efficient approach to function similarity can assist in typical reverse engineering tasks.
title_zh: 实用的类型推断：高吞吐量恢复现实世界中的结构与函数签名
abstract_zh: 从剥离的二进制文件中恢复类型是实现精确反编译的关键，但其实际应用仍面临诸多挑战。对于复合结构而言，不仅需要准确的布局信息，还需保持语义一致性，才能实现端到端的完整重建。现有许多方法要么仅合成布局，要么在事后推断名称，这削弱了后续使用的实用性。这一问题在自动化环境中尤为严重，因为其带来的运行时开销过高，难以承受。我们提出 XTRIDE，一种改进的基于 n-gram 的方法，专注于实用性：通过高度优化的吞吐量和可操作的置信度评分，该方法可直接部署于自动化流水线中。与当前最先进的结构体恢复技术相比，我们的方法在性能相当的前提下，速度提升了 70 到 2300 倍。由于我们的推理基于真实世界中的类型数据，因此实现了最高比例的完全正确结构布局。通过优化训练策略，我们在 DIRT 数据集上的表现超越当前最优水平 5.09 个百分点，整体类型推断准确率达到 90.15%。此外，我们还证明了基于 n-gram 的类型预测具有良好的泛化能力，可应用于函数签名恢复：通过对嵌入式固件进行案例研究，我们展示了这种高效的方法在函数相似性分析中的优势，能够有效支持典型的逆向工程任务。

title: AI Agents, Language, Deep Learning and the Next Revolution in Science
authors: Ke Li, Beijiang Liu, Bruce Mellado, Changzheng Yuan, Zhengde Zhang
abstract: Modern science is reaching a critical inflection point. Instruments across disciplines, from particle physics and astronomy to genomics and climate modeling, now produce data of such scale, diversity, and interdependence that traditional analytical methods can no longer keep pace. This growing imbalance between data generation and data understanding signals the need for a new scientific paradigm. We propose that intelligent, human-supervised AI agents operating over deep-learning algorithms, represent the next evolution of the scientific method. Built upon large language models and multimodal learning, these agents can interpret scientific intent, design and execute analytical workflows, and ensure traceability through domain-specific languages that preserve human oversight and accountability. Particle physics, a historic incubator of computational innovation, offers the ideal testbed for this transition. At the Institute of High Energy Physics of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, the Dr. Sai system embodies this vision, a multi-agent reasoning framework deployed within collider research at the CEPC. This emerging approach does not replace human scientists but extends their cognitive reach, enabling discovery to scale with complexity and redefining how knowledge itself is produced in the age of intelligent machines. The significance of this paradigm transcends particle physics, offering a blueprint for all data-driven sciences facing the same complexity ceiling.
title_zh: 人工智能代理、语言、深度学习与科学的下一次革命
abstract_zh: 现代科学正面临一个关键的转折点。从粒子物理、天文学到基因组学和气候模拟，各学科的仪器所产生的数据规模空前庞大、类型多样且相互关联，传统分析方法已无法跟上数据生成的速度。这种数据产生与理解能力之间的日益失衡，昭示着需要一种新的科学范式。我们提出，基于深度学习算法、由人类监督的智能人工智能代理，将代表科学方法的下一次演进。这些代理依托于大型语言模型和多模态学习技术，能够理解科学意图，自主设计并执行分析流程，并通过领域特定语言确保可追溯性，从而保障人类的监督与责任。作为计算创新的历史摇篮，粒子物理为这一转型提供了理想的试验场。在中国科学院高能物理研究所，名为“赛博士”（Dr. Sai）的系统正是这一愿景的体现——它是一个部署于CEPC对撞机研究中的多智能体推理框架。这种新兴方法并非取代人类科学家，而是拓展其认知边界，使发现能力能够随复杂性的增长而提升，重新定义智能机器时代知识生产的方式。这一范式的深远意义超越了粒子物理，为所有面临相同复杂性瓶颈的数据驱动型科学提供了可借鉴的蓝图。

title: Reasoning as Compression: Unifying Budget Forcing via the Conditional Information Bottleneck
authors: Fabio Valerio Massoli, Andrey Kuzmin, Arash Behboodi
abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting improves LLM accuracy on complex tasks but often increases token usage and inference cost. Existing "Budget Forcing" methods reducing cost via fine-tuning with heuristic length penalties, suppress both essential reasoning and redundant filler. We recast efficient reasoning as a lossy compression problem under the Information Bottleneck (IB) principle, and identify a key theoretical gap when applying naive IB to transformers: attention violates the Markov property between prompt, reasoning trace, and response. To resolve this issue, we model CoT generation under the Conditional Information Bottleneck (CIB) principle, where the reasoning trace Z acts as a computational bridge that contains only the information about the response Y that is not directly accessible from the prompt X. This yields a general Reinforcement Learning objective: maximize task reward while compressing completions under a prior over reasoning traces, subsuming common heuristics (e.g., length penalties) as special cases (e.g., uniform priors). In contrast to naive token-counting-based approaches, we introduce a semantic prior that measures token cost by surprisal under a language model prior. Empirically, our CIB objective prunes cognitive bloat while preserving fluency and logic, improving accuracy at moderate compression and enabling aggressive compression with minimal accuracy drop.
title_zh: 推理即压缩：通过条件信息瓶颈统一预算强制机制
abstract_zh:
思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）提示能够提升大语言模型在复杂任务上的准确性，但通常会增加 token 使用量和推理成本。现有的“预算强制”方法通过引入启发式长度惩罚进行微调以降低计算开销，却同时抑制了必要的推理过程与冗余的填充内容。我们从信息瓶颈（Information Bottleneck, IB）原理出发，将高效推理重新建模为一种有损压缩问题，并识别出将朴素 IB 方法直接应用于 Transformer 模型时的一个关键理论缺陷：注意力机制破坏了提示（prompt）、推理过程（reasoning trace）与最终响应（response）之间的马尔可夫性质。

为解决这一问题，我们基于条件信息瓶颈（Conditional Information Bottleneck, CIB）原则对思维链生成进行建模，其中推理过程 Z 作为计算桥梁，仅保留从提示 X 无法直接获取、但对响应 Y 至关重要的信息。由此导出一个通用的强化学习目标：在最大化任务奖励的同时，对生成结果进行压缩，且压缩过程遵循对推理路径的先验分布。该框架将常见的启发式策略（如长度惩罚）统一为特例（例如，均匀先验对应于简单的长度惩罚）。与依赖 token 数量统计的传统方法不同，我们引入了一种语义先验，通过语言模型先验下的“意外性”（surprisal）来衡量 token 成本。

实验结果表明，我们的 CIB 目标能够在保持流畅性和逻辑性的同时有效消除认知冗余（cognitive bloat），在中等压缩率下提升准确率，并实现激进压缩时几乎无损的性能表现。

title: Agentic Neurosymbolic Collaboration for Mathematical Discovery: A Case Study in Combinatorial Design
authors: Hai Xia, Carla P. Gomes, Bart Selman, Stefan Szeider
abstract: We study mathematical discovery through the lens of neurosymbolic reasoning, where an AI agent powered by a large language model (LLM), coupled with symbolic computation tools, and human strategic direction, jointly produced a new result in combinatorial design theory. The main result of this human-AI collaboration is a tight lower bound on the imbalance of Latin squares for the notoriously difficult case $n \equiv 1 \pmod{3}$. We reconstruct the discovery process from detailed interaction logs spanning multiple sessions over several days and identify the distinct cognitive contributions of each component. The AI agent proved effective at uncovering hidden structure and generating hypotheses. The symbolic component consists of computer algebra, constraint solvers, and simulated annealing, which provides rigorous verification and exhaustive enumeration. Human steering supplied the critical research pivot that transformed a dead end into a productive inquiry. Our analysis reveals that multi-model deliberation among frontier LLMs proved reliable for criticism and error detection but unreliable for constructive claims. The resulting human-AI mathematical contribution, a tight lower bound of $4n(n{-}1)/9$, is achieved via a novel class of near-perfect permutations. The bound was formally verified in Lean 4. Our experiments show that neurosymbolic systems can indeed produce genuine discoveries in pure mathematics.
title_zh: 代理式神经符号协作在数学发现中的应用：组合设计领域的案例研究
abstract_zh: 我们通过神经符号推理的视角研究数学发现，其中由大型语言模型（LLM）驱动的AI代理，结合符号计算工具与人类的战略引导，共同在组合设计理论领域取得了一项新成果。此次人机协作的主要成果是针对一个长期棘手的情形 $n \equiv 1 \pmod{3}$，给出了拉丁方不平衡性的紧下界。我们基于跨越数日多个会话的详细交互日志，重构了整个发现过程，并识别出各组成部分的独特认知贡献：AI代理在揭示隐藏结构和生成假设方面表现出色；符号计算部分包括计算机代数、约束求解器和模拟退火算法，提供了严格的验证与穷尽枚举；而人类的引导则提供了关键的研究转向，将一度陷入僵局的探索转变为富有成效的研究路径。我们的分析表明，前沿大语言模型之间的多模型讨论在批评与错误检测方面表现可靠，但在提出建设性主张方面则不可靠。最终的人机数学贡献是一个紧下界 $4n(n{-}1)/9$，该结果通过一类新颖的近完美置换实现。该界限已在 Lean 4 中完成形式化验证。实验表明，神经符号系统确实能够在纯数学领域实现真正的发现。

title: Capacity-Aware Mixture Law Enables Efficient LLM Data Optimization
authors: Jingwei Li, Xinran Gu, Jingzhao Zhang
abstract: A data mixture refers to how different data sources are combined to train large language models, and selecting an effective mixture is crucial for optimal downstream performance. Existing methods either conduct costly searches directly on the target model or rely on mixture scaling laws that fail to extrapolate well to large model sizes. We address these limitations by introducing a compute-efficient pipeline for data mixture scaling. First, we propose CAMEL, a capacity-aware mixture law that models validation loss with the nonlinear interplay between model size and mixture. We also introduce a loss-to-benchmark prediction law that estimates benchmark accuracy from validation loss, enabling end-to-end performance prediction for the target model. Next, we study how to allocate a fixed compute budget across model scales to fit the law and reduce prediction error. Finally, we apply our method to Mixture-of-Experts models with up to 7B-A150M parameters to fit the law, and verify the optimal mixture derived from the law by extrapolating to a 55B-A1.2B target model. Compared to prior methods, we reduces mixture optimization costs by 50\% and improves downstream benchmark performance by up to 3\%.
title_zh: 容量感知的混合定律实现大语言模型数据优化的高效性
abstract_zh: 数据混合指的是在训练大型语言模型时，如何将不同数据源进行组合。选择有效的数据混合方式对于实现下游任务的最优性能至关重要。现有的方法要么直接在目标模型上进行代价高昂的搜索，要么依赖于混合缩放定律，但这些定律在扩展到大模型规模时外推效果不佳。为解决这些问题，我们提出了一种计算高效的**数据混合缩放**流程。首先，我们提出了CAMEL（容量感知混合定律），该定律通过建模模型规模与混合之间的非线性相互作用，来预测验证损失。同时，我们引入了一种从验证损失预测基准性能的损失-基准预测定律，从而实现对目标模型端到端性能的精准预测。接着，我们研究了如何在固定计算预算下，合理分配不同模型规模的资源以拟合该定律并降低预测误差。最后，我们将该方法应用于参数量达7B-A150M的专家混合（Mixture-of-Experts）模型，成功拟合了该定律，并通过外推至55B-A1.2B的目标模型，验证了所推导出的最优混合方案的有效性。相比以往方法，我们的方法将混合优化成本降低了50%，同时在下游基准测试中性能提升最高达3%。

title: SWE-Fuse: Empowering Software Agents via Issue-free Trajectory Learning and Entropy-aware RLVR Training
authors: Xin-Cheng Wen, Binbin Chen, Haoxuan Lan, Hang Yu, Peng Di, Cuiyun Gao
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have transformed the software engineering landscape. Recently, numerous LLM-based agents have been developed to address real-world software issue fixing tasks. Despite their state-of-the-art performance, Despite achieving state-of-the-art performance, these agents face a significant challenge: \textbf{Insufficient high-quality issue descriptions.} Real-world datasets often exhibit misalignments between issue descriptions and their corresponding solutions, introducing noise and ambiguity that mislead automated agents and limit their problem-solving effectiveness. We propose \textbf{\textit{SWE-Fuse}}, an issue-description-aware training framework that fuses issue-description-guided and issue-free samples for training SWE agents. It consists of two key modules: (1) An issue-free-driven trajectory learning module for mitigating potentially misleading issue descriptions while enabling the model to learn step-by-step debugging processes; and (2) An entropy-aware RLVR training module, which adaptively adjusts training dynamics through entropy-driven clipping. It applies relaxed clipping under high entropy to encourage exploration, and stricter clipping under low entropy to ensure training stability. We evaluate SWE-Fuse on the widely studied SWE-bench Verified benchmark shows to demonstrate its effectiveness in solving real-world software problems. Specifically, SWE-Fuse outperforms the best 8B and 32B baselines by 43.0\% and 60.2\% in solve rate, respectively. Furthermore, integrating SWE-Fuse with test-time scaling (TTS) enables further performance improvements, achieving solve rates of 49.8\% and 65.2\% under TTS@8 for the 8B and 32B models, respectively.
title_zh: SWE-Fuse：通过无问题轨迹学习与熵感知的RLVR训练赋能软件代理
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）已经彻底改变了软件工程领域。近期，众多基于LLM的智能体被开发出来，以应对现实世界中的软件问题修复任务。尽管这些智能体取得了顶尖的性能表现，但仍面临一个关键挑战：**高质量问题描述的严重不足**。真实世界的数据集往往存在问题描述与对应解决方案之间的不一致，这种错位引入了噪声和歧义，误导自动化智能体，限制其解决问题的有效性。

为此，我们提出了**SWE-Fuse**——一种面向问题描述感知的训练框架，通过融合受问题描述引导的样本与无问题描述的样本，来训练软件工程智能体。该框架包含两个核心模块：(1) **无问题描述驱动的轨迹学习模块**，在缓解潜在误导性问题描述的同时，使模型能够学习逐步调试的过程；(2) **基于熵感知的RLVR训练模块**，通过熵驱动的裁剪机制自适应地调整训练动态：在高熵情况下采用宽松裁剪以鼓励探索，在低熵情况下采用严格裁剪以保障训练稳定性。

我们在广泛研究的SWE-bench Verified基准上对SWE-Fuse进行了评估，结果充分证明了其在解决真实世界软件问题方面的有效性。具体而言，SWE-Fuse在8B和32B规模的基线模型中，分别将求解率提升了43.0%和60.2%。此外，将SWE-Fuse与测试时缩放（TTS）相结合，可进一步提升性能，在TTS@8设置下，8B和32B模型的求解率分别达到49.8%和65.2%。

title: SynPlanResearch-R1: Encouraging Tool Exploration for Deep Research with Synthetic Plans
authors: Hansi Zeng, Zoey Li, Yifan Gao, Chenwei Zhang, Xiaoman Pan, Tao Yang, Fengran Mo, Jiacheng Lin, Xian Li, Jingbo Shang
abstract: Research Agents enable models to gather information from the web using tools to answer user queries, requiring them to dynamically interleave internal reasoning with tool use. While such capabilities can in principle be learned via reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), we observe that agents often exhibit poor exploration behaviors, including premature termination and biased tool usage. As a result, RLVR alone yields limited improvements. We propose SynPlanResearch-R1, a framework that synthesizes tool-use trajectories that encourage deeper exploration to shape exploration during cold-start supervised fine-tuning, providing a strong initialization for subsequent RL. Across seven multi-hop and open-web benchmarks, \framework improves performance by up to 6.0% on Qwen3-8B and 5.8% on Qwen3-4B backbones respectively compared to SOTA baselines. Further analyses of tool-use patterns and training dynamics compared to baselines shed light on the factors underlying these gains. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/HansiZeng/syn-plan-research.
title_zh: SynPlanResearch-R1：通过合成计划鼓励工具探索以进行深度研究
abstract_zh: 研究型代理（Research Agents）能够通过使用工具从网络上获取信息，以回答用户查询，这要求它们在内部推理与工具使用之间动态地交替进行。尽管这类能力原则上可通过具有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）来学习，但我们观察到，这些代理常常表现出较差的探索行为，包括过早终止和工具使用的偏差。因此，仅依靠RLVR带来的改进十分有限。为此，我们提出了SynPlanResearch-R1框架，该框架通过合成促进更深层次探索的工具使用轨迹，在冷启动的监督微调阶段塑造探索行为，为后续的强化学习提供强有力的初始化。在七个涉及多跳推理和开放网络的基准测试中，相较于当前最优基线方法，\framework 在 Qwen3-8B 模型上性能提升最高达 6.0%，在 Qwen3-4B 模型上提升最高达 5.8%。对工具使用模式和训练动态的进一步分析，揭示了这些性能提升背后的关键因素。我们的代码已公开，地址为 https://github.com/HansiZeng/syn-plan-research。

title: Human-AI Collaboration for Scaling Agile Regression Testing: An Agentic-AI Teammate from Manual to Automated Testing
authors: Moustapha El Outmani, Manthan Venkataramana Shenoy, Ahmad Hatahet, Andreas Rausch, Tim Niklas Kniep, Thomas Raddatz, Benjamin King
abstract: Agile organizations increasingly rely on automated regression testing to sustain rapid, high-quality software delivery. However, as systems grow and requirements evolve, a persistent bottleneck arises: test specifications are produced faster than they can be transformed into executable scripts, leading to mounting manual effort and delayed releases. In partnership with Hacon (a Siemens company), we present an agentic AI approach that generates system-level test scripts directly from validated specifications, aiming to accelerate automation without sacrificing human oversight. Our solution features a retrieval-augmented, multi-agent architecture integrated into Hacon's agile workflows. We evaluate this system through a mixed-method analysis of industrial artifacts and practitioner feedback. Results show that the AI teammate significantly increases test script throughput and reduces manual authoring effort, while underscoring the ongoing need for clear specifications and human review to ensure quality and maintainability. We conclude with practical lessons for scaling regression automation and fostering effective Human-AI collaboration in agile environments.
title_zh: 人机协作实现敏捷回归测试的规模化：从手动测试到自动化测试的智能AI团队成员
abstract_zh: 敏捷组织越来越依赖自动化回归测试，以持续实现快速、高质量的软件交付。然而，随着系统规模扩大和需求不断演进，一个持续存在的瓶颈逐渐显现：测试规范的生成速度远超其转化为可执行脚本的速度，导致手动工作量不断增加，发布周期被延迟。在与西门子旗下公司Hacon的合作中，我们提出了一种基于智能体（agentic AI）的方法，能够直接从经过验证的规格说明生成系统级测试脚本，旨在加速自动化进程的同时，不牺牲人工监督。我们的解决方案采用检索增强型多智能体架构，并无缝集成到Hacon的敏捷工作流程中。通过混合方法分析工业实践文档与从业者反馈，我们对系统进行了评估。结果表明，AI协作伙伴显著提升了测试脚本的产出效率，大幅减少了手动编写的工作量；同时强调了清晰规范和人工审查对于保障质量与可维护性的持续重要性。最后，我们总结出在敏捷环境中规模化回归自动化以及促进高效人机协作的实用经验与启示。
